sections.paragraph:
ἀναδίδωμι fut. 3 sg. ἀναδώσει Sir 1:22; 2 aor. ἀνέδωκα Just.; 2 aor. ptc. ἀναδούς; pf. 3 sg. ἀναδέδωκε Just., A II, 2, 8 (Pind. et al.; ins, pap; Sir 1:23; Joseph., Just.) deliver, hand over τινί τι (Philo, Aet. M. 62; Jos., Ant. 1, 249; βιβλίδιον Just., A I, 29, 2; A II, 2, 8) ἀ. τὴν ἐπιστολὴν τῷ ἡγεμόνι Ac 23:33 (the same bureaucratese in Polyb. 29, 10, 7; Diod S 11, 45, 3; IG XIV, 830, 22; PTebt 448; PFay 130, 15). Of the transmission of an apology to Hadrian Qua (1).—M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. pf. = perfect

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

Qua Qua = Quadratus fragment, II a.d.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 62
